blocks.body: Evaluating an expression means substituting given values for the variables and calculating the result using the order of operations (PEMDAS).
Steps: (1) Write the expression. (2) Substitute the given value(s) for each variable. (3) Simplify using PEMDAS.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: Evaluate 2a^2 - 3b when a = 3 and b = -2. Substitute: 2(3)^2 - 3(-2) = 2(9) + 6 = 18 + 6 = 24.

blocks.label: Example 3
blocks.body: Evaluate (x + y)^2 when x = 5 and y = -1. Substitute: (5 + (-1))^2 = (4)^2 = 16.
Always use parentheses when substituting negative numbers to avoid sign errors. Write -3 as (-3), not just -3.
